name: L. A. Electrical Workers Credit Un.
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving IBEW Local 11 electrical workers since 1953, offering savings accounts, loans, and financial services with competitive rates.
description_long:
L.A. Electrical Workers Credit Union was established in 1953 and has operated as a member-owned financial institution for over 70 years. As a credit union rather than a bank, it is owned by its members rather than external shareholders, which shapes its operational philosophy and service approach.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including share savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, and multiple loan types. Loan products include auto loans (new and used vehicles at 4.99% APR), motorcycle loans (5.55% APR), RV and watercraft financing (7.99% APR), signature loans (10.79% APR), and share-secured loans (3.40% APR). Additional services include insurance products, debt protection, vacation funds, financial calculators, and online banking with a mobile app in development.

The institution distinguishes itself through member-centric governance—members elect the Board of Directors and Supervisory Committee rather than external investors controlling decisions. Membership requires affiliation with IBEW Local 11 (or family relationship to a member) and only a $20 minimum deposit plus $5 one-time membership fee. The credit union emphasizes competitive rates, fewer fees, and higher yields on savings compared to traditional banks, with family members eligible to join under specific circumstances.

For eligible members within the electrical workers union, this is a legitimate local financial institution with transparent operations. The main limitation is membership restriction to IBEW Local 11 members and their families, which excludes the general public. The website provides basic information but lacks detailed fee schedules and specific product terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: New and used auto loans (4.99% APR)
pros: Member-owned structure means profits return to members through better rates and lower fees rather than external shareholders
Auto loans as low as 4.99% APR and share-secured loans at 3.40% APR are competitively priced
Minimal membership barrier at $20 minimum deposit and $5 one-time fee
Family members of IBEW Local 11 members can join without requiring union dues receipts
No-fee checking account (share draft) available for members
Established 70-year operating history demonstrates stability and longevity
Members elect board representation providing democratic governance voice
cons: Membership restricted to IBEW Local 11 electrical workers and their immediate family—general public cannot join
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, terms and conditions, and specific product disclosures
No physical branch location information or hours of operation provided on website
Limited online functionality with mobile app still listed as 'coming soon'
No information provided about insurance coverage limits, deposit insurance, or member protections
best_for: IBEW Local 11 electrical union members seeking competitive loan rates and member-focused banking
Spouses and children of union members eligible for family membership accounts
Members prioritizing lower fees and higher savings yields over convenience features
Individuals seeking auto/motorcycle financing with rates below commercial bank average
diagnosis: L.A. Electrical Workers Credit Union is appropriate for IBEW Local 11 members and their families seeking competitive rates on loans and savings products with member-owned governance. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—non-union individuals and families not connected to Local 11 cannot open accounts, making this exclusively available to a specific occupational group.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
